items.title: AI agents that actually work
items.body: Crossed the threshold from neat demo to genuinely useful. Claude, the tool ecosystem, the way it reshapes a workday. I am using agents every day now and feeling the compounding.

items.title: Down the mechanical keyboard rabbit hole
items.body: Tactile feedback, sound profile, keycap sets. Went in skeptical, came out with a HHKB-layout board and opinions about switches.

items.title: Inversion
items.body: Instead of asking 'how do I succeed?' ask 'how do I fail?' Then avoid those things. Stupid simple, incredibly powerful.

items.title: Framework Laptop
items.body: A laptop you can actually repair and upgrade yourself. Modular ports, replaceable everything. This is how all tech should be made.

items.title: Raycast
items.body: Spotlight replacement on steroids. Snippets, window management, calculator, endless extensions. Mac only, worth buying a Mac for.

items.title: The observable universe
items.body: There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on Earth. And that's just the part we can see. What the actual fuck.

items.title: Obsidian
items.body: Where my second brain lives. Markdown files, local storage, insane plugin ecosystem. The graph view is beautiful but honestly I never use it.

items.title: Compound interest in skills
items.body: Getting 1% better every day means you're 37x better after a year. The math checks out and it completely reframed how I think about learning.

items.title: Third wave coffee
items.body: Once you go specialty, you can't go back to Starbucks. Learning about origins, processing methods, and roast profiles is its own rabbit hole.

items.title: Claude Code
items.body: The AI coding assistant that actually understands context. Built this website with it. Yes, we're in a recursive situation here.

items.title: The best time to plant a tree
items.body: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. This proverb lives rent-free in my head whenever I think about starting something new.

items.title: Figma dev mode
items.body: Still the gold standard for design. Dev mode update made handoff so much smoother. Plus it runs in a browser which is wild.

items.title: Standing desk setup
items.body: Took me 3 iterations to get it right. Standing mat, monitor arms, cable management. Your back will thank you.

items.title: You are the average
items.body: You become the average of the 5 people you spend most time with. Choose wisely. This made me audit my relationships ruthlessly.

items.title: Lex Fridman Podcast
items.body: Three-hour conversations with the most interesting people on the planet. Put it on 1.5x and go for a long walk.

items.title: Veritasium
items.body: Derek makes science videos that make you feel smarter. Production quality insane, not afraid to go deep on complex topics.

items.title: Not Boring by Packy McCormick
items.body: Best business/tech newsletter out there. Deep dives into companies and trends that make you smarter about the world.
